articles.headline: JetBlue Passenger With Highest Mosaic Status Caught Performing Inappropriate Act Inflight

articles.takeaway: Frequent flyer elite status provides no immunity from enforcement of inflight conduct standards, reinforcing that premium cabin privileges carry equal responsibility for passenger behavior compliance.

articles.headline: After 20 Years, The Airbus A380 Still Shapes Airport Design
articles.blurb: A closer look at some of the upgrades that airports had to make in order to accommodate the superjumbo.
articles.takeaway: The A380's infrastructure legacy demonstrates how aircraft design decisions create decades-long economic commitments for airports, influencing terminal layouts and gate configurations long after production ends.

articles.headline: 5 Airlines With The Largest Boeing 787 Fleets In 2026
articles.blurb: The aircraft is becoming the backbone of many airlines' fleets.
articles.takeaway: The 787's dominance in long-haul fleets signals a structural shift toward fuel-efficient widebody operations that will reshape route profitability and competitive dynamics through the decade.

articles.headline: Safety Culture in Aviation SMS
articles.blurb: How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.
articles.takeaway: Organizations with mature safety cultures achieve higher SMS effectiveness through psychological safety that encourages voluntary hazard reporting rather than blame-driven compliance.

articles.takeaway: Starfish Space's ability to secure alternative partnerships quickly demonstrates the growing ecosystem of companies willing to collaborate on orbital servicing missions.

articles.blurb: The aircraft offers exceptional long-range capabilities.
articles.takeaway: A350's structural efficiency enables airlines to profitably operate ultra-long routes previously requiring larger widebodies, reducing seat-mile costs on thin markets.

articles.headline: New Global Aviation Rules On Power Banks Come Into Effect – Here's What's Changing
articles.blurb: Passengers can now carry up to two power banks each onboard.
articles.takeaway: The two-power-bank allowance reflects industry efforts to balance passenger convenience with cargo fire-risk mitigation, reducing operational burden compared to previous restrictions.

articles.headline: Why Do Some Airlines Still Operate 4-Engine Jets?
articles.blurb: Airlines mostly operate legacy quadjets because they are affordable or they have no other choice. Emirates is unusual for just loving its A380s.
articles.takeaway: Four-engine aircraft persistence reflects economics—used quadjets offer lower acquisition costs than retiring aging fleets, despite higher fuel consumption than modern twins.

articles.headline: USMC F-35Cs and USS Tripoli ARG Arrive in the Middle East

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational optimization across all aviation layers offers immediate sustainability gains without waiting for next-generation aircraft, addressing the critical gap between rising demand and environmental constraints.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: NASA Selects Intuitive Machines to Deliver Artemis Science, Tech to Moon
articles.blurb: NASA has awarded Intuitive Machines of Houston, $180.4 million to deliver NASA-funded science and technology to the lunar surface as part of the agency’s CLPS (Commercial Lunar Payload Services) initiative and Artemis program. This lunar delivery, which includes seven payloads — five of them NASA’s...
articles.takeaway: NASA's $180.4M contract with Intuitive Machines accelerates commercial lunar logistics capability essential for sustained Artemis operations beyond crewed missions.

articles.headline: Commercial Space Federation (CSF) Welcomes Two New Associate Members

articles.headline: ESA to decide by June on Europe’s Gateway contributions

articles.takeaway: NASA's Gateway halt forces Europe to reassess its €1+ billion lunar infrastructure commitment, potentially reshaping international lunar exploration partnerships beyond 2025.

articles.headline: NISAR’s View of Mount Rainier
articles.blurb: Description This image captured by U.S.-Indian Earth satellite NISAR on Nov. 10, 2025, shows Washington’s Mount Rainier. The image is cropped from a much larger swath spanning the Pacific Northwest on a cloudy day; NISAR’s L-band SAR instrument is able to peer through the clouds at the surface...
articles.takeaway: SAR technology's ability to penetrate cloud cover enables continuous Earth observation for disaster preparedness and infrastructure monitoring, critical for aviation weather and terrain mapping.

articles.headline: NTSB Issues Preliminary Report on Hudson River Ditching
articles.blurb: Investigators say an instructional flight ended after a loss of oil pressure and total engine failure.

articles.headline: House Transportation Chair Sam Graves To Retire
articles.blurb: Graves helped shape FAA reauthorization, BasicMed and several other general aviation measures.
articles.takeaway: Loss of a key congressional advocate for general aviation could slow progress on pending regulatory reforms and industry-friendly policies during critical FAA reauthorization discussions.
